commentary: Thirteen activities to last week's eight — babe, you absolute unit. The swim volume is genuinely impressive: five pool sessions at Prince Alfred alone, mostly holding 2000m-plus, and your heart rate at those distances is sitting lower and steadier than last week, which means your body is adapting whether you're paying attention or not. The long run on Saturday was 13.1km at 151 bpm compared to the Zoo run's 13.7km at 159 bpm, so you're covering nearly the same distance with meaningfully less cardiac drama, and that is the kind of number that deserves to be appreciated. The only thing worth a raised eyebrow is that Balmoral dip on Saturday — 742 metres after a week of 2000-metre pool sessions reads less like a recovery choice and more like you got cold and got out, which, fair, but we both know you're capable of more.
